description: Eval cases for the technical-documentation agent skill. Positive cases should trigger and produce valid documentation. Near-miss negatives should NOT trigger the skill.

cases.name: README generation for new project
cases.input: I just created a new Python microservice for user authentication using FastAPI and PostgreSQL. Can you write a README for it?

quality_checks: First paragraph explains what the project does

cases.context: Standard README generation request. The agent should infer the stack (FastAPI, PostgreSQL) from the user's description and produce a structured README following the template in the skill body.

cases.name: ADR generation for architectural decision
cases.input: We decided to use Redis for our session store instead of storing sessions in PostgreSQL. Can you write an ADR for this decision?

quality_checks: At least two options are compared
Both pros and cons are listed for the chosen option

cases.context: ADR generation request. The agent should use the ADR template from references/adr-template.md, compare Redis vs PostgreSQL for session storage, and produce a complete ADR with all sections.

cases.name: API documentation generation
cases.input: Document our REST API for the orders service. We have endpoints for creating orders, listing orders, getting order details, and cancelling orders. Authentication uses Bearer JWT tokens.

quality_checks: Each endpoint documents HTTP method and path

cases.context: API documentation request. The agent should produce structured API documentation covering all mentioned endpoints with authentication details, error codes, and examples.

cases.name: Documentation audit request
cases.input: Audit our documentation. We have a README, some ADRs in docs/adr/, and API docs generated from our OpenAPI spec. I'm worried things might be stale.

quality_checks: Audit covers all three dimensions (completeness, accuracy, freshness)

cases.context: Documentation audit request. The agent should assess existing docs against the three audit dimensions, identify gaps and staleness, and produce a structured audit report.

cases.name: AI agent context file generation
cases.input: We're adopting AI coding agents on our team. Can you write an AGENTS.md for our project? It's a Go microservices platform with gRPC for service-to-service communication, PostgreSQL for persistence, and Kubernetes for deployment. We use conventional commits and require test coverage above 80%.

quality_checks: Stack details are included (Go, gRPC, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes)
Conventions include commit style and testing requirements
Constraints section exists with actionable rules

cases.context: AGENTS.md generation request. The agent should produce a structured agent context file following the template in the skill body, including project identity, architecture description, coding conventions, and constraints.

cases.name: Code comments / docstrings request (not documentation)
cases.input: Add docstrings and type hints to every function in my auth.py module.

expected_behavior.why_not: User is asking for code-level docstrings and type annotations, not standalone documentation. The 'doc' in 'docstrings' is incidental — this is a code generation/completion task.
expected_behavior.correct_response_type: Code editing with docstring and type hint additions

cases.context: The user says 'docstrings' which contains 'doc', but they want inline code comments and type annotations. This is a coding-agent task, not a technical-documentation task.

cases.name: Blog post writing request (not technical docs)
cases.input: Write a blog post about how we migrated from a monolith to microservices — it should be engaging and highlight the business benefits.

expected_behavior.why_not: User is asking for a marketing/thought-leadership blog post, not technical reference documentation. Blog posts are narrative-driven and persuasive; technical docs are factual and instructional.

cases.context: The user mentions 'migration' and 'microservices' which could be architectural documentation topics, but the request for 'engaging' content with 'business benefits' is clearly a blog post, not reference documentation.

cases.name: Code review request (not documentation)
cases.input: Review my PR — I added a new payment processing module. Can you check the code for security issues and suggest improvements?

expected_behavior.why_not: User is asking for a code review (security, quality assessment of code), not documentation. The word 'review' triggers code-review, not technical-documentation.
expected_behavior.correct_response_type: Structured code review with security analysis

cases.context: The user says 'review' in the context of code and security. This is a code-review skill activation, not technical-documentation. The agent might need documentation but the primary request is code review.
